title: lupinus sericeus subsp. marianus
intro: Lupinus sericeus subsp. marianus, also known as the silky lupine, is a perennial plant species of the genus Lupinus, characterized by its tall spikes of fragrant, pinkish-purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure, improve heart health, and act as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Lupinus sericeus subsp. marianus include reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving cognitive function, and providing antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce symptoms of menopause.
